title: Bier conformance case
description: Black-box conformance case shape per docs/AGENT_PLAN.md §5.1. One YAML per scenario (request -> response). Authored by the Spec Researcher, consumed by the Tester's conformance runner. This schema is intentionally permissive about the body-assertion key (body_exact / body_jsonpath / body_contains) so areas that assert in different styles all validate; exactly one body assertion style SHOULD be present per case. Two request shapes are supported: an HTTP request (method + path, the common case) and a CLI invocation (kind: cli + flag) used by the config/observability areas that exercise --dump-config and startup validation rather than the HTTP surface.

config.description: Optional PostgREST config the case requires. The conformance runner applies these before running the request and restores defaults after. Top-level keys mirror PostgREST config names; their values are strings, booleans, numbers, or null (null clears a setting, e.g. db-anon-role: null). The reserved keys `env`, `file`, and `preconditions_sql` carry, respectively, a PGRST_*/PGUSER environment map, a config-file key/value map, and a list of SQL statements to run before the invocation (used by the config area's --dump-config cases).

env.description: Environment variables (PGRST_*, PGUSER, ...) set for the invocation.

file.description: Config-file key/value pairs (canonical or deprecated-alias keys).

preconditions_sql.description: SQL statements run before the invocation (e.g. ALTER ROLE ... SET pgrst.* for db-config cases).

id.description: Globally-unique numeric case id. Each feature area owns a non-overlapping band (e.g. ordering owns 1200..1249).

request.description: Either an HTTP request (method + path) or a CLI invocation (kind: cli + flag). HTTP is the default shape; CLI is used by the config/observability --dump-config and startup-validation cases.

kind.description: Request shape selector. Absent (or `http`) means an HTTP request; `cli` means a command-line invocation of the PostgREST binary.

flag.description: CLI flag/argument for a `kind: cli` invocation, e.g. "--dump-config".

path.description: Request path including query string, exactly as sent on the wire.

headers.description: Request headers as a string->string map.

jwt.description: Instructs the runner to mint a signed JWT and send it as the Bearer token. `sign_with` names a key/secret fixture; `payload` is the claim set to sign. Used by the auth area to exercise specific claim shapes.

body.description: Optional request body (object, array, or raw string).

body_json.description: Optional request body asserted to be sent as JSON (object or array). Alternative to `body` when the case wants to make the JSON intent explicit.

body_raw.description: Optional raw (non-JSON) request body sent verbatim, e.g. a CSV payload or an empty string for a no-body POST.

schema.description: Which spec/conformance/fixtures/<name>.sql to load before running this case.

preconditions.description: Optional SQL statements to run before the request.

expect.description: Assertions on the outcome. HTTP cases assert `status` (+ headers/body); CLI cases assert `exit_code` (+ dump_contains / stderr_contains). At least one of `status` or `exit_code` MUST be present.

exit_code.description: Expected process exit code for a CLI invocation: 0, a specific non-zero integer, or the literal string "nonzero" when only failure (not a specific code) is asserted.

stderr_contains.description: Substring(s) the process stderr must contain (CLI startup-validation cases).

dump_reparse_stable.description: When true, the dumped config re-fed and re-dumped must be byte-identical (idempotence check).

status_text.description: Expected HTTP reason phrase. Used when a case asserts a custom status text, e.g. the RAISE SQLSTATE 'PGRST' DETAIL status_text override (errors area).

headers.description: Expected response headers as a string->string map.

body_exact.description: Literal JSON document the body must equal (object, array, scalar, or null when no body assertion).

body_jsonpath.description: List of JSONPath assertions; each entry is matched against the response body.

body_contains.description: Substring(s) the raw response body must contain (for non-JSON bodies, e.g. CSV).

body_raw.description: Literal raw (non-JSON) response body the response must equal verbatim, e.g. a CSV document.

body_json.description: Literal JSON document the body must equal (object or array). Synonym of body_exact used when the case wants to make the JSON intent explicit.

headers_absent.description: List of response header names that MUST be absent (e.g. Content-Type on an empty-body 201/204). Mirrors PostgREST's matchHeaderAbsent assertions.

headers_present.description: List of response header names that MUST be present (value unconstrained). Mirrors PostgREST's matchHeaderExists.

headers_match.description: Map of header name -> regular expression the header value must match (e.g. the Server-Timing dur=<ms> pattern). Mirrors PostgREST's matchHeader regex assertions.

headers_absent_in_value.description: Map of header name -> list of substrings that MUST NOT appear within that header's value (e.g. plan/transaction absent from an OPTIONS Server-Timing value).

headers_no_blank.description: When true, asserts the response carries no header with an empty name or empty value (regression for blank ` : ` headers).

notes.description: Free-text rationale; SHOULD reference the upstream PostgREST it-block.

source.description: Source URL with a line anchor, pinned to the PostgREST version under test. MUST use raw.githubusercontent.com for machine-fetchability.
